package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for migration_or_seed_logic_callable_from_request_handlers.
// Rule intent: Request handlers that can trigger migration or seed workflows.
// Anti-pattern: Handler directly calls migration/seed functions.

type User struct {
	ID       uint   `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	Name     string `gorm:"column:name"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id"`
}

func RunMigrations(db *gorm.DB) error {
	return db.AutoMigrate(&User{})
}

func SeedData(db *gorm.DB) error {
	return db.Create(&User{Name: "admin", TenantID: "default"}).Error
}

func HandleAdminAction(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	action := c.Param("action")

	switch action {
	case "migrate":
		if err := RunMigrations(db); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"status": "migrations applied"})

	case "seed":
		if err := SeedData(db); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"status": "data seeded"})

	default:
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "unknown action"})
	}
}
